name: Majá Robledo Trail
name_original: Mirador de la Pregonera desde Casarrubia
description: Hike the moderate 4.6-mile Majá Robledo Trail in Las Hurdes, gaining 935 feet with stunning views from Pregonera Viewpoint.
region_intro: Hiking around Casares De Las Hurdes offers exploration of a mountainous region characterized by winding terrain, deep valleys, and abundant natural water features. The landscape is shaped by successive mountain ranges and rivers like the Hurdano, Ladrillar, and Malvellido, which carve impressive meanders. This area in northern Cáceres, Extremadura, provides a diverse environment for outdoor activities, with numerous waterfalls and natural pools. Best hiking trails around Casares De Las Hurdes The most popular hiking route is El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco, an easy 1.6 miles (2.6 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete, leading to a notable waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Majá Robledo Trail, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through the local Hurdanian landscape. Local hikers also love the Pregonera Viewpoint loop from Casares de las Hurdes, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Casares De Las Hurdes is defined by chaotic mountainous terrain, intricate valleys, and meandering rivers. The network offers options for easy walks and moderate trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Casares De Las Hurdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Casares De Las Hurdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Casares De Las Hurdes?
A: The region is characterized by its chaotic and winding mountainous terrain, intricate valleys, and abundant natural water features. You'll encounter impressive river meanders, such as the Meandros del Malvedillo visible from Mirador de El Gasco, and powerful waterfalls like the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4511250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990362286/el-chorro-de-la-meancera-waterfall-from-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; trail is an easy 2.6 km walk, typically taking about an hour. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clearing by the River – El Gasco loop from La Meancera&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 2.7 km.

Q: Where can I find impressive viewpoints in the area?
A: The region offers several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4767629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pregonera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of the Hurdano River Valley and the surrounding mountain ranges. Additionally, the Meandro del Melero, though slightly outside the immediate area, offers a spectacular panoramic view from the Mirador La Antigua within Las Hurdes.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990362286/el-chorro-de-la-meancera-waterfall-from-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy hike directly to the impressive Chorro de la Meancera. This waterfall is best visited from autumn to spring for the strongest flow.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Casares De Las Hurdes?
A: The region is generally suitable for hiking year-round. However, for experiencing the waterfalls at their most powerful, autumn to spring is ideal due to higher water flow. The Fuente del Colla'o Hiking Route, for example, is described as transitable year-round.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hiking options available?
A: For those seeking a longer experience, the Alfonso XIII Path (PR-CC 212 Senda de Alfonso XIII) is a 22 km footpath connecting Casares de las Hurdes with Las Mestas. The region also features sections of larger hiking networks like the GR10 and GR22.3. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pregonera Viewpoint loop from Casares de las Hurdes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.6 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find natural pools for swimming after a hike?
A: Yes, the clear waters of the rivers in Las Hurdes form natural pools. Charco de La Olla in Las Mestas, fed by the Ladrillar river, is a notable spot offering opportunities for wild swimming.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Casares De Las Hurdes?
A: The hiking routes in Casares De Las Hurdes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the well-maintained paths that lead to impressive natural features.

Q: Are there any unique geological sites to explore?
A: Yes, the El Gasco Volcano is a unique geological feature in the area. It is famed for being formed by a meteorite impact two million years ago, with the presence of ringwoodite making it a global rarity.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pregonera Viewpoint loop from Casares de las Hurdes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.6 km circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de la Meancera – Clearing by the River loop from Fragosa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.4 km loop.

Q: What kind of cultural or historical points of interest can be found along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, hikers can discover ancient settlements, such as the remains of a Bronze Age settlement near El Gasco, and rock engravings. The region also features unique slate architecture in its small farmhouses, reflecting a rich cultural heritage.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Casares De Las Hurdes and the surrounding villages typically offer parking options for visitors. It is advisable to check local signage upon arrival or consult specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.
tips: Nice panoramic point very close to the border with Salamanca and that allows you to clearly appreciate the picturesque landscape of the north of Extremadura. A route that can be started in Casarrubia, which can be easily reached by car.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Majá Robledo Trail?
faqs.answer: The Majá Robledo Trail is rated as moderate. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 285 meters over its 7.4-kilometer length. Hikers can expect rugged desert mountain terrain, including limestone cliffs and arroyo bottoms, which may present some strenuous sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: You will hike through a diverse Chihuahuan Desert landscape. The terrain is characterized by rugged desert mountains, limestone cliffs, narrow canyons, and arroyo bottoms. Expect to see unique geological formations, including slot canyons, and desert plants like juniper and creosote.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight on this route is the Pregonera Viewpoint, offering stunning vistas. Additionally, the wider Robledo Mountains area is known for its unique geological formations, including slot canyons, and the nearby Prehistoric Trackways National Monument, which features 280-million-year-old fossilized footprints.

faqs.question: Is the Majá Robledo Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, the trail is generally suitable for hikers with some experience. Beginners who are physically fit and prepared for varied desert terrain and elevation changes may find it manageable, but it's not an easy stroll.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for hiking in the Robledo Mountains, including this trail, are typically spring and fall. During these times, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activity. Summer can be extremely hot, and winter mornings can be chilly, though daytime temperatures are often pleasant.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Majá Robledo Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Majá Robledo Trail are not provided, but trails in the Robledo Mountains area generally have designated trailheads with parking. It's advisable to check local land management websites (e.g., BLM) for the most current information on trailhead access and parking facilities near Las Cruces, New Mexico.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Majá Robledo Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not detailed, dogs are generally permitted in the Robledo Mountains Wilderness and the Prehistoric Trackways National Monument area. It is crucial to keep dogs on a leash at all times and to pack out all waste to protect the delicate desert ecosystem and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Majá Robledo Trail?
faqs.answer: Currently, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Majá Robledo Trail or accessing the general Robledo Mountains Wilderness area. However, always check with the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) Las Cruces District Office for any updated regulations or requirements before your visit, especially if planning to visit the Prehistoric Trackways National Monument.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Majá Robledo Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the desert environment, essential items include plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen, sunglasses), sturdy hiking shoes, and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as well as snacks and a first-aid kit. Be prepared for rugged terrain and potential exposure to the elements.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for hiking in the Robledo Mountains?
faqs.answer: Yes, safety is paramount in the desert. Always carry ample water, inform someone of your hiking plans, and be aware of potential wildlife such as snakes and scorpions. Flash floods can occur in arroyos during heavy rains, so check weather forecasts. Stick to marked trails to avoid getting lost and to protect sensitive desert flora and fauna.
